revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「{{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][名西郡]][[[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]<b…」
revision.text:
{{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][名西郡]][[[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[石井町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]]

{{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[石井町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603） {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= |所在地= [[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん

東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。神名帳には阿波国名方郡の多祁御奈刀祢神社とあり、祢の字を弥に誤伝していると思われる。 正しくは、タケミナカタ・ミタマヤ神社と推定される。 漢字を誤伝した上にタケミナトミと意味のわからない読みをさせている。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 この分霊による元諏訪の表記をこともあろうか由緒書きにて信濃國一之宮 諏訪大社の元社であるとしている。このようなはずかしい由緒が読める神社は阿波以外にはないだろう。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『[[日本歴史地名体系]] 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{注意|このページの参考文献節は適正でない可能性があります。|date=2018年1月}} {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する[[神社]]である。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「[[光仁天皇|光仁帝]]の[[宝亀]]十未年（779年）、[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]][[南方刀美神社]]（現在の[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]）は、[[阿波国]]名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、[[タケミカヅチ|武御雷神]]と争った[[建御名方神]]の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{注意|このページの参考文献節は適正でない可能性があります。|date=2018年1月}} {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する[[神社]]である。式内社。[[諏訪大社]]の元社。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「[[光仁天皇|光仁帝]]の[[宝亀]]十未年（779年）、[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]][[南方刀美神社]]（現在の[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]）は、[[阿波国]]名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、[[タケミカヅチ|武御雷神]]と争った[[建御名方神]]の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{参照方法|date=February 2018}} {{神社 |name=Take Minato Jinja |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |address={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |別名=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |例祭=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its annual festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]][[Category:Suwa Faith topics in the template that need translation to English]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

During its annual festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Former Gōsha shrines]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Suwa Faith]][[Category:Suwa Faith]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:original pages]][[Category:original pages]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{神社 |name=Take Minato Jinja |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}}[[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{神社 |name=Take Minato Jinja |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place Name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:Https://ja.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{神社 |name=Take Minato Jinja |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{神社 |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:bump 1-20 (2)]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[Category:Ugoodbye $1 Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha}}, Nagano Prefecture)." trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] imported pages]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}}

revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}}

{{注意|このページの参考文献節は適正でない可能性があります。|date=2018年1月}} {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |所在地= [[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪213-1 |祭神= [[建御名方命]]
[[八坂刀売命]] |社格= [[式内社]]・[[旧郷社]] |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= [[11月3日]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[名西郡]][[石井町]]浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する[[神社]]である。式内社。[[諏訪大社]]の元社。 == 歴史 == 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は[[1720年]]（[[享保]]5年）に建築された。 [[徳島藩]]主である[[蜂須賀家]]からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。[[1627年]]（[[寛永]]4年）には[[蜂須賀光隆]]が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、[[鮎喰川]]の出水に遮られることがあるため、[[徳島城]]から近い[[諏訪神社 (徳島市)|諏訪神社]]に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「[[光仁天皇|光仁帝]]の[[宝亀]]十未年（779年）、[[信濃国]][[諏訪郡]][[南方刀美神社]]（現在の[[長野県]][[諏訪大社]]）は、[[阿波国]]名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、[[タケミカヅチ|武御雷神]]と争った[[建御名方神]]の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 == 祭神 == * [[建御名方命]] * [[八坂刀売命]] == 境内社 == * 地神社 == 交通 == * [[四国旅客鉄道|JR]][[徳島線]][[下浦駅]]より徒歩で約25分。同線[[牛島駅]]より徒歩約20分。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == 参考文献 == * 『[[角川日本地名大辞典]] 36 徳島県』（[[1986年]] ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『[[日本歴史地名大系]] 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:Ambox transclusions]] {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}}
