revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|qq=draft|qq=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|qq=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishii, Tokushima|qq=}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1720|qq=}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka clan|qq=}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1627|qq=}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Mitsutaka|qq=}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Castle|qq=}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hōki|qq=}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōnin|qq=}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Taisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa-taisha|qq=}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimoura Station|qq=}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokushima Line|qq=}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ushijima Station|qq=}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]]

Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location=[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Myōzai District, Tokushima|Myōzai District]], [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa Shrines]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]]

revision.text:
{{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location=[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Myōzai District, Tokushima|Myōzai District]], [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|lt=Ken-sha|1=Modern_system_of_ranked_Shinto_shrines#District_shrine|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * revision.text:
[[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q48759404]] Wikidata name: Takeminatomi-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg |location=[[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]], [[:en:Myōzai District, Tokushima|Myōzai District]], [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii]], Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima |location = {{ill|Ishii|qid=Q1205943}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072361|N|134.411472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ishii]] {{wikidata link|Q48759404}}

{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima |location = {{ill|Ishii|WD=Q1205943}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072361|N|134.411472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{translated page|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|version=101721916|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii Town]] [[Category:Municipal History of Ishii]] {{wikidata link|Q48759404}}

Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|1=Yasakatome|2=ja|3=八坂刀売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 八坂刀売神}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gōsha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[:en:November 3|November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima |location = {{ill|Ishii|WD=Q1205943}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.072361|N|134.411472|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Ishii, Tokushima|Ishii, Tokushima]], Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]]. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in [[:en:Kyōhō|1720]] 5. The shrine was highly revered by the [[:en:Hachisuka clan|Hachisuka clan]], feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In [[:en:Kan'ei|1627]] 4, [[:en:Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Hachisuka Mitsutaka]] prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|qq=}} near [[:en:Tokushima Castle|Tokushima Castle]] for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of [[:en:Hōki|Hōki]] during the reign of Emperor [[:en:Emperor Kōnin|Kōnin]] (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day [[:en:Suwa-taisha|Suwa-taisha]], Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9150391}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takemikazuchi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タケミナカタ|1=Takeminakata|comment=changed title based on * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Earth God Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from [[:en:Shimoura Station|Shimoura Station]] on the [[:en:Tokushima Line|Tokushima Line]] (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from [[:en:Ushinoshima Station|Ushijima Station]] on the same line. == References == === Notes === == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of shrines]] [[Category:Suwa faith]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of revision.comment: Text replacement - "is a shrine in the Engishiki Jinmyōchō." to "is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]."

{{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588,
