(Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}}[[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:2]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:3]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] revision.text:
[[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} [[sn:2]] [[sn:3]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} [[sn:2]] [[sn:3]] [[sn:4]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[sn:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[sn:agimitonotoya Shrine]] [[en:Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} ==Notes== [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tagimitonotoya Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} ==Notes== [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tagimitonotoya Shrine]] [[ja:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Gō-sha]] [[Category:History of Ishii, Tokushima]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|建御名方命}}
{{jalink|八坂刀売命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= {{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=多祁御奈
刀弥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。式内社・多祁御奈刀祢神社（たけみなとねじんじゃ）の論社。{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}の元社などの説もある。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1720年|Q6908}}（{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}5年）に建築された。 {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。式内社・多祁御奈刀祢神社（たけみなとねじんじゃ）の論社。{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}の元社などの説もある。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1720年|Q6908}}（{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}5年）に建築された。 {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not
