Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} ==Notes== [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tagimitonotoya Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005001222 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県名西郡石井町浦庄字諏訪213-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Japanese content == {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|建御名方命}}
{{jalink|八坂刀売命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= {{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=多祁御奈
刀弥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。式内社・多祁御奈刀祢神社（たけみなとねじんじゃ）の論社。{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}の元社などの説もある。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1720年|Q6908}}（{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}5年）に建築された。 {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|建御名方命}}

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。式内社・多祁御奈刀祢神社（たけみなとねじんじゃ）の論社。{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}の元社などの説もある。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1720年|Q6908}}（{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}5年）に建築された。 {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} ==Notes== [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tagimitonotoya {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= 多祁御奈刀弥神社 |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|建御名方命}}
{{jalink|八坂刀売命}} |社格= {{jalink|式内社}}・旧{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}} |創建= 不詳 |本殿= |別名= お諏訪さん
東の宮 |例祭= {{jalink|11月3日|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=多祁御奈
刀弥神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''多祁御奈刀弥神社'''（たけみなとみじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|徳島県|Q160734}}{{jalink|名西郡|Q1155732}}{{jalink|石井町|Q1205943}}浦庄字諏訪に鎮座する{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}である。式内社・多祁御奈刀祢神社（たけみなとねじんじゃ）の論社。{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}の元社などの説もある。 === 歴史 === 創建年は不詳。現在の社殿は{{jalink|1720年|Q6908}}（{{jalink|享保|Q1145048}}5年）に建築された。 {{jalink|徳島藩|Q3034557}}主である{{jalink|蜂須賀家}}からの尊崇が高く、御例祭の際には参拝・代参していた。{{jalink|1627年|Q6833}}（{{jalink|寛永|Q1192913}}4年）には{{jalink|蜂須賀光隆|Q3125458}}が祈願し奇瑞を著した。また、蜂須賀氏が参拝の際は、{{jalink|鮎喰川|Q11674107}}の出水に遮られることがあるため、{{jalink|徳島城|Q8011246}}から近い{{jalink|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|Q11631927|諏訪神社}}に分霊したとも伝わる。 同神社の社伝記によると、「{{jalink|光仁天皇|Q958662|光仁帝}}の{{jalink|宝亀|Q18520676}}十未年（779年）、{{jalink|信濃国|Q289250}}{{jalink|諏訪郡|Q1131473}}{{jalink|南方刀美神社}}（現在の{{jalink|長野県|Q127877}}{{jalink|諏訪大社|Q218813}}）は、{{jalink|阿波国|Q907457}}名方郡諏訪大明神を移遷し奉る」（『浦庄村史』）とある。つまり、国譲り交渉で、{{jalink|タケミカヅチ|Q1502690|武御雷神}}と争った{{jalink|建御名方神}}の本源地は、阿波国の名方郡諏訪村（旧高足（タカシ）郷）で、長野県の南方刀美神社（諏訪大社）は、記紀の成立後に、阿波から移遷勧請されたことが、神社の記録に遺っていた笹田孝至『皇都ヤマトは阿波だった』サンロータス研究所2024年、p.173-185。 === 祭神 === * {{jalink|建御名方命}} * {{jalink|八坂刀売命}} === 境内社 === * 地神社 === 交通 === * {{jalink|四国旅客鉄道|Q496531|JR}}{{jalink|徳島線|Q899310}}{{jalink|下浦駅|Q7497021}}より徒歩で約25分。同線{{jalink|牛島駅|Q11570130}}より徒歩約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist|2}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === 参考文献 === * 『{{jalink|角川日本地名大辞典|Q4093011}} 36 徳島県』（{{jalink|1986年|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603）p.440 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『{{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 37 徳島県の地名 』（2000年 ISBN 4582490379) p.545 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 * 『徳島県百科事典』（1981年 徳島新聞社調査事業局 編）p.588 【多祁御奈刀弥神社】の項 {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not revision.comment: AI-assisted translation: Japanese wiki content translated by local AI agents (deepseek-r1:8b via Ollama, reviewed/assembled by Claude Code). Quality varies — some pages may contain subtle translation errors.
revision.text:

It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|lt=Q3029647|WD=Q3029647}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|lt=Q17|WD=Q17}} == name in kana (P1814) == * たけみなとみじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5480005001222 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '徳島県名西郡石井町浦庄字諏訪213-1', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:多祁御奈刀弥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Japanese content == {{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}{{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}}, {{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{jalink|Tokushima Domain|Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{jalink|1627|Q6833}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 4), {{jalink|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{jalink|Ayukui River|Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|Suwa Shrine}} near {{jalink|Tokushima Castle|Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{jalink|Emperor Kōnin|Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} (today’s {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} in {{jalink|Nagano Prefecture|Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimoura Station|Q7497021}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Line|Q899310}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: used local AI agents to translate these pages. Contrary to the description it was actually Claude Code for a few, OpenClaw with Codex, and regular Codex. Ollama and DeepSeek were too bad to actually use. see categorization since some of them are very questionable, especially if there was a massive decrease in page size or the {{jawiki| template is minimal or absent on the page

{{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}{{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}}, {{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{jalink|Tokushima Domain|Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{jalink|1627|Q6833}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 4), {{jalink|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{jalink|Ayukui River|Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|Suwa Shrine}} near {{jalink|Tokushima Castle|Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{jalink|Emperor Kōnin|Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} (today’s {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} in {{jalink|Nagano Prefecture|Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimoura Station|Q7497021}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Line|Q899310}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]] [[Category:need translation]]

About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]]

{{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}{{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}{{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}}
{{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} |社格= {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}・former {{jalink|gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{jalink|November 3|Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{jalink|Ishii|Q1205943}}, {{jalink|Myōzai District|Q1155732}}, {{jalink|Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{jalink|1720|Q6908}} ({{jalink|Kyōhō|Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{jalink|Tokushima Domain|Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{jalink|1627|Q6833}} ({{jalink|Kan'ei|Q1192913}} 4), {{jalink|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{jalink|Ayukui River|Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{jalink|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|Suwa Shrine}} near {{jalink|Tokushima Castle|Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{jalink|Emperor Kōnin|Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{jalink|Shinano Province|Q289250}} (today’s {{jalink|Suwa Taisha|Q218813}} in {{jalink|Nagano Prefecture|Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{jalink|Awa Province|Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{jalink|Takemikazuchi|Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{jalink|Takeminakata-no-Mikoto}} * {{jalink|Yasakatome-no-Mikoto}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Shimoura Station|Q7497021}} on the {{jalink|Tokushima Line|Q899310}} of {{jalink|JR Shikoku|Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{jalink|Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{jalink|1986|Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]]

{{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill||lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}
{{ill||lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill||lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} * {{ill||lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{参照方法|date=2018年2月}} {{神社 |名称= Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=q|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=q|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Take Minato Jinja}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=q|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たけみなとみしんしや}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:諏訪神社]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]
