|image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

|image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404|1=Take Minato Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404|1=Take Minato Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404|1=Take Minato Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, "Take Minato Jinja." {{shinto-stub}} ==References== {{reflist}} {{Suwa Faith}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

revision.text:
'''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | — || {{ill|Tagimitonotoya Shrine|lt=Takeminatomi Shrine|ja|多祁御奈刀弥神社|WD=Q48759404|1=Take Minato Shrine}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized|1=Kokuhei-sha}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shōsha|WD=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} || — || — || {{coord|34.072398|134.411463|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=183204 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q48759404}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{注意|This article's references section may not comply with proper standards.|date=January 2018}} {{Citation style|date=February 2018}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Take Minato Jinja | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Place name Series, Volume 37: Tokushima Prefecture* (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia* (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Department), p.588, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]][[Category:Qq but with jawiki]]

During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} == Bibliography == * *Kadokawa Japanese Place name Dictionary, Volume 36: Tokushima Prefecture* (1986, ISBN 4040013603), p.440, "Take Minato Jinja." * *Japanese Historical Tagimitonotoya Shrine |画像= [[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|250px]] |所在地= {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}{{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}} Ura-shō, Aza Suwa 213-1 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神= {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格= {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}・former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建= Unknown |本殿= |別名= Osuwa-san
Higashi-no-Miya |例祭= {{ill|lt=November 3|Q2995|WD=Q2995|1=November 3}} |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=Tagi Mina
toya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tagimitonotoya Shrine''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} enshrined in Ura-shō, Aza Suwa, {{ill|lt=Ishii|Q1205943|WD=Q1205943|1=Ishii, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Myōzai District|Q1155732|WD=Q1155732|1=Myōzai District, Tokushima}}, {{ill|lt=Tokushima Prefecture|Q160734|WD=Q160734|1=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a candidate shrine for the shikinaisha Take-minatone Shrine. There is also a theory that it is the original shrine of {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}}. === History === The founding year is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|lt=1720|Q6908|WD=Q6908|1=1720}} ({{ill|lt=Kyōhō|Q1145048|WD=Q1145048|1=Kyōhō}} 5). It was deeply revered by the Hachisuka family, lords of the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Domain|Q3034557|WD=Q3034557|1=Tokushima Domain}}, who worshiped in person or by proxy at the annual grand festival. In {{ill|lt=1627|Q6833|WD=Q6833|1=1627}} ({{ill|lt=Kan'ei|Q1192913|WD=Q1192913|1=Kan'ei}} 4), {{ill|lt=Hachisuka Mitsutaka|Q3125458|WD=Q3125458|1=Hachisuka Mitsutaka}} prayed and reportedly received auspicious signs. It is also said that because visits by the Hachisuka could be blocked by flooding of the {{ill|lt=Ayukui River|Q11674107|WD=Q11674107|1=Akui River}}, a divided spirit was enshrined at {{ill|lt=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima)|Q11631927|WD=Q11631927|lt=Suwa Shrine|1=Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)}} near {{ill|lt=Tokushima Castle|Q8011246|WD=Q8011246|1=Tokushima Castle}}. According to the shrine’s traditional record, “in Hōki 10 (779), under {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōnin|Q958662|WD=Q958662|1=Emperor Kōnin}}, Minamikatotomi Shrine in Suwa District, {{ill|lt=Shinano Province|Q289250|WD=Q289250|1=Shinano Province}} (today’s {{ill|lt=Suwa Taisha|Q218813|WD=Q218813|1=Suwa-taisha}} in {{ill|lt=Nagano Prefecture|Q127877|WD=Q127877|1=Nagano Prefecture}}) transferred and enshrined Suwa Daimyōjin from Myōkata District, {{ill|lt=Awa Province|Q907457|WD=Q907457|1=Awa Province (Tokushima)}}” (''Ura-shō Village History''). In other words, this record preserves a view that the original base of Takeminakata, who contended with {{ill|WD=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi|Q1502690|WD=Q1502690|1=Takemikazuchi}} in the kuni-yuzuri episode, was in Awa, and that Suwa Taisha in Nagano was invited from Awa after the compilation of the KikiSasada Takashi, ''The Imperial Capital Yamato Was Awa'', Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp.173-185.. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Mikoto|1=Takeminakata|WD=Q9150391}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yasakatome-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} === Precinct shrine === * Chigami Shrine === Access === *About 25 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Shimoura Station|Q7497021|WD=Q7497021|1=Shimoura Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Tokushima Line|Q899310|WD=Q899310|1=Tokushima Line}} of {{ill|lt=JR Shikoku|Q496531|WD=Q496531|1=Shikoku Railway Company}}. About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Ushinoshima Station|Q11570130|WD=Q11570130|1=Ushinoshima Station}} on the same line. === Notes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Annotations === {{Notelist|2}} === References === {{Reflist|colwidth=30em}} === Bibliography === * ''{{ill|lt=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten|Q4093011|WD=Q4093011|1=Kadokawa Nihon Chimei Daijiten}} 36 Tokushima Prefecture'' ({{ill|lt=1986|Q2430|WD=Q2430|1=1986}} ISBN 4040013603), p.440, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''{{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 37 Place Names of Tokushima Prefecture'' (2000, ISBN 4582490379), p.545, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” * ''Tokushima Prefecture Encyclopedia'' (1981, Tokushima Shimbunsha Research Bureau ed.), p.588, “Tagimitonotoya Shrine.” {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tagimitonotoya Shrine]][[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Suwa Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:石井町の歴史]] [[Category:石井町の建築物]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Suwa shrines]]

Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources === |image=[[File:Take Minato-jinja (Ishii,Tokushima).jpg|280px]] |location={{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Myōzai District|ja|名西郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ishii|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Urashō Aza Suwa 213-1 |deity={{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} |type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Former {{ill|Gōsha|ja|郷社|12=draft|12=draft}} |創建=Unknown |本殿= |alternative names=O-Suwa-san
Higashi no Miya |festival=[[November 3]] |神事= |緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 4 | 緯度秒 = 20.5 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N |経度度 = 134 |経度分 = 24 | 経度秒 = 41.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E |地図国コード = JP |地図 = Japan Tokushima }} '''Take Minato Jinja''' (たけみなとみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Ishii, Tokushima|ja|石井町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Tokushima Prefecture, Japan. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and regarded as an ancestral shrine of {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}. == History == The year of the shrine's founding is unknown. The current shrine building was constructed in {{ill|1720|ja|享保|12=draft|12=draft}} 5. The shrine was highly revered by the {{ill|Hachisuka clan|ja|蜂須賀氏|12=draft|12=draft}}, feudal lords of the Tokushima Domain. During its festival, members of the clan would often personally visit or send representatives. In {{ill|1627|ja|寛永|12=draft|12=draft}} 4, {{ill|Hachisuka Mitsutaka|ja|蜂須賀光隆|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed at the shrine and reported miraculous occurrences. Additionally, a division of the shrine's spirit was transferred to {{ill|Suwa Shrine (Tokushima City)|ja|諏訪神社 (徳島市)|12=draft|12=draft}} near {{ill|Tokushima Castle|ja|徳島城|12=draft|12=draft}} for easier access during times when the flooding of the Ayukui River prevented direct visits to Take Minato Jinja. According to the shrine's historical records, "In the tenth year of {{ill|Hōki|ja|宝亀|12=draft|12=draft}} during the reign of Emperor {{ill|Kōnin|ja|光仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} (779 CE), the Suwa Daimyōjin of Nakata District, Awa Province, was transferred and enshrined at the Minato Shrine in Suwa, Shinano Province (modern-day {{ill|Suwa Taisha|ja|諏訪大社|1=Suwa-taisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, Nagano Prefecture)." This implies that {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}}, known for his mythological dispute with {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|武御雷神|12=draft|12=draft}}, originally hailed from Awa Province and that Suwa Taisha's origins trace back to Awa, as recorded in the shrine's documents.Sasada Takashi, *The Imperial Capital Yamato was Awa*, Sun Lotus Research Institute, 2024, pp. 173-185. == Deities == * {{ill|Takeminakata no Mikoto|ja|建御名方命|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Yasakatome no Mikoto|ja|八坂刀売命|12=draft|12=draft}} == Auxiliary Shrines == * {{ill|Earth God Shrine|ja|地神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == Access == * Approximately a 25-minute walk from {{ill|Shimoura Station|ja|下浦駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the {{ill|Tokushima Line|ja|徳島線|12=draft|12=draft}} (JR Shikoku). * Approximately a 20-minute walk from {{ill|Ushijima Station|ja|牛島駅|12=draft|12=draft}} on the same line. == References == === Notes === {{Notelist|2}} === Sources ===
